title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
SEO Insertion on How Does Group Term Life Insurance Work? - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: group term life insurance, group term insurance, group term insurance plan
Body Content:
Without question, employees of any organisation are considered the most precious assets. Employers feel a sense of responsibility for the well-beingof their staff, and this sentiment extends to families of employees too. In the unfortunate case of an employee’s accidental demise, the employee’s family is adversely affected, both mentally and financially. Such a case becomes even worse if the employee was the sole breadwinner of the household.
The crucial role that any employer can play in a situation such as this is to assure an employee’s family of financial protection in the absence of the employee. The financial help that group term life insurance provides may not be sufficient to tide over the total cost burden of any family, but it can act as an immediate buffer. Group term life insurance is a good way for employers to provide a death benefit to an employee's beneficiaries, while motivating employees to remain in employment.
What is a Group Term Life insurance cover?
The name says a lot about group term insurance. A policy under this plan can be provided to a group of individuals. Commonly, these are employer-employee group contracts, but such a policy could be used for groups other than employees. Companies are required to have a minimum capacity of staff to avail plans that fall within the purview of group insurance. Such policies and plans cover all employees with a single assured sum of cover. The cover for each employee within a specified group is determined, based on a range of variables like marital status, number of members in the family, staff designation, etc.
Who pays the premium for the policy?
While availing of any insurance policy, the premiums must be paid regularly, and are based on factors regarding individual employees. However, in a group term insurance plan, one single premium is paid for the group policy as a whole. Employers generally make premium payments, and there is an option for employees to top up this base amount from salaries on a monthly basis.
The values of premiums are not set, and these depend on the size of any organisation and the average ages of employees covered. Also, members contained under a group policy can be removed or added according to different circumstances during the course of the year. Additionally, group term life insurance offers many benefits. Firstly, it is cost-effective for employers. It also acts as a way to provide employees benefits for beneficiaries in their absence. More employer benefits come with a group plan as the liability for gratuity is simple to fund under a group plan.
How to Avail Group Life Insurance
In order to avail of group life insurance, specific steps must be followed. Not only is the process of how to avail it important, but the claim process needs to be known. Here are the relevant steps to be aware of:
Decide your Assured Sum - From the point of view of the employer, the full assured sum depends on how cover is offered. If you are an employer, consider your company’s objectives and its structure while offering group term insurance. Here are the kinds of cover employers can offer:
Flat Coverage - This is the same cover given to all employees, ideal for startups and small firms.
Graded Coverage - This kind is perfect for large companies with a hierarchical structure.
Salary Multiples - This kind of coverage is suitable for companies that make use of a range of compensation packages for employees at the same level.
You can also select a lump sum as the assured sum.
Choose Riders - You can add riders as supplementary benefits to any group plan. For instance, critical illness cover is one rider. Besides these, you may also provide certain options to settle home or car loans of employees, should the demise of an employee occur.
Estimate of Premium - Once you have come to a decision on the final assured sum with rider benefits in any group term insurance plan, insurers will typically give you an estimate of the premium. You should note that premiums for basic policies will be lower than those that include additional riders.
Premium Payment and Policy Issuance - Group plans are issued to employers after the premium for the policy is paid. The tenure is fixed at one year. Your master group policy may be renewed after a year, and renewed premiums will depend on the reduction or addition of employees and any claims raised. Once the master policy is ready, after employers have submitted appropriate documentation and the premium amount, it is given to the employer or the company administrator. The policy document should be read thoroughly to assess risk coverage.
How to File a Claim Under Group Term Life Insurance
In the event of the unfortunate demise of an employee, the employer must raise a claim with the insurer, meeting the requirements (including document submission) below:
Insurance claim form
Insurance certificate
Original certificate of death
Present address proof of the beneficiaries/claimant
Photo ID proof of claimant
Bank statement of the claimant
The insurer will evaluate the claim and provide a due date for claim settlement. Once the claim is settled, the employee’s name is removed from the policy.
Conclusion
It is possible for beneficiaries of a group insurance plan to monitor the status of any claims made. Moreover, such plans help employers to retain staff and attract talent as this is a benefit, and aid employees to be stress-free in terms of financial protection for their families. In addition to group term insurance, a health insurance plan is also vital, as well as an individual life insurance plan.
full_pdf_text:
How Does Group Term Life Insurance Work? Without question, employees of any organisation are considered the most precious assets. Employers feel a sense of responsibility for the well-being of their staff, and this sentiment extends to families of employees too. In the unfortunate case of an employee’s accidental demise, the employee’s family is adversely affected, both mentally and financially. Such a case becomes even worse if the employee was the sole breadwinner of the household. The crucial role that any employer can play in a situation such as this is to assure an employee’s family of financial protection in the absence of the employee. The financial help that group term life insurance provides may not be sufficient to tide over the total cost burden of any family, but it can act as an immediate buffer. Group term life insurance is a good way for employers to provide a death benefit to an employee's nominees, while motivating employees to remain in employment. What is a Group Term Life insurance cover? The name says a lot about group term insurance. A policy under this plan can be provided to a group of individuals. Commonly, these are employer-employee group contracts, but such a policy could be used for groups other than employees. Companies are required to have a minimum capacity of staff to avail plans that fall within the purview of group insurance. Such policies and plans cover all employees with a single assured sum of cover. The cover for each employee within a specified group is determined, based on a range of variables like marital status, number of members in the family, staff designation, etc. Who pays the premium for the policy? While availing of any insurance policy, the premiums must be paid regularly, and are based on factors regarding individual employees. However, in a group term insurance plan, one single premium is paid for the group policy as a whole. Under Employer- Employee group insurance scheme employers generally make premium payments, and there is an option for employees to top up this base amount from salaries on a monthly basis. The values of premiums are not set, and these depend on the size of any organisation and the average ages of employees covered. Also, members contained under a group policy can be removed or added according to different circumstances during the course of the year. How to Avail Group Life Insurance In order to avail group life insurance, specific steps must be followed. Here are the relevant steps to be aware of:
1. Decide your Assured Sum - From the point of view of the employer, the full assured sum
depends on how cover is offered. If you are an employer, consider your company’s
objectives and its structure while offering group term insurance. 1Few of the coverages based on the applicability to an organization :  Flat Coverage - This is the same cover given to all employees, suitable for startups and small firms.
● Graded Coverage - This kind is perfect for large companies with a hierarchical structure. ● Salary Multiples - This kind of coverage is suitable for companies that make use of a range of compensation packages for employees at the same level.
2. Choose Riders - You can add riders as supplementary benefits to any group plan. For
instance, critical illness cover is one rider. Besides these, you may also provide certain options to settle home or car loans of employees, should the demise of an employee occur.
3. Estimate of Premium - Once you have come to a decision on the final assured sum with rider
benefits in any group term insurance plan, insurers will typically give you an estimate of the premium. You should note that premiums for basic policies will be lower than those that include additional riders.
4. Premium Payment and Policy Issuance - Group plans are issued to employers after the
premium for the policy is paid. The tenure is fixed at one year. Your master group policy may be renewed after a year, and renewed premiums will depend on the reduction or addition of employees and any claims raised. Once the master policy is ready, (after employers have submitted appropriate documentation and the premium amount), it is given to the employer or the Master policy holder as the case may be. The policy document should be read thoroughly to assess risk coverage. Conclusion Group Insurance plans under employer-employee scheme are one of the ways to help employers to retain staff and attract talent as this is a benefit, and aid employees to be stress-free in terms of financial protection for their families. In addition to group term insurance, an individual life insurance plan may also be considered by the employees depending on their requirements. Source:
